hawkish_phrases: monetary policy could need to be tightened by a somewhat greater extent over the forecast period than current market expectations
some withdrawal of monetary stimulus is likely to be appropriate over the coming months
spare capacity in the economy is being absorbed a little more rapidly than expected
inflation remains likely to overshoot the 2% target over the next three years
Two members considered it appropriate to increase Bank Rate by 25 basis points
dovish_phrases: any prospective increases in Bank Rate would be expected to be at a gradual pace and to a limited extent
seven members thought that the current policy stance remained appropriate
regular pay growth remains modest in the near term
There remain considerable risks to the outlook
stands ready to respond to changes in the economic outlook
summary: The MPC voted 7-2 to hold Bank Rate at 0.25%, with a clear hawkish bias as a majority judged that some withdrawal of monetary stimulus is likely to be appropriate over the coming months, and two members dissented in favour of an immediate 25bp hike.
